epistemic_boundary.tests: Whether a candidate system preserves a declared theory-derived causal organization at a specified level of analysis.
does_not_test: Whether the system is phenomenally conscious
Whether the system has personhood or moral status
Whether a consciousness theory is globally correct

system_roles.requirement: Implements the declared mechanism graph and candidate mapping.
Matches evaluator-visible outputs through an alternative pathway that omits at least one declared essential dependency.
Matches compute, memory, bandwidth, latency, and iteration budget without preserving the declared topology.
Derived from A by a targeted intervention while preserving neighboring capacities as far as feasible.

behavioral_equivalence.rule: A comparison is not behaviorally matched when any preregistered primary matching metric exceeds the declared tolerance without adjustment or exclusion.
behavioral_equivalence.residual_policy: Model residual differences explicitly; never treat imperfect matching as exact equivalence.

candidate_mapping_schema.rule: Shared terminology, behavioral similarity, or architectural resemblance alone cannot satisfy a mapping.

intervention_families.purpose: Remove or disable one mapped component while preserving adjacent resources.
Replace a mapped component with a behaviorally competent alternative lacking the target dependency.
Estimate whether the mapped pathway mediates the predicted downstream effect.
Transfer, restore, scramble, or counterfactually replace internal states across conditions.
Create a controlled mismatch between the proposed internal state and the report or behavior attributed to it.
Test whether the declared dependency generalizes beyond the matching distribution.

score_definition.2: Explicit mapping with material unresolved mismatches.
score_definition.3: Explicit runnable mapping with bounded mismatches and preregistered predictions.

score_definition.0: Targeted interventions fail or effects are nonselective.
score_definition.2: Replicable selective effect with some unresolved capacity confounding.
score_definition.3: Replicable selective effect surviving capacity and sham controls.

score_definition.0: Reports or behavior are independent of the mapped state.
score_definition.2: State substitution changes predicted outcomes with partial specificity.
score_definition.3: Multiple counterfactual interventions recover the preregistered dependency matrix.

score_definition.0: Behavioral theater reproduces the full indicator profile.
score_definition.1: Theater control fails only on evaluator-sensitive measures.
score_definition.2: Theater control fails on some hidden-state-linked measures.
score_definition.3: Theater control fails on a preregistered cross-domain mechanistic battery.
Effect survives preregistered compute, memory, bandwidth, and latency controls.

score_definition.0: Effect reverses with probe or prompt changes.
score_definition.3: Stable across blinded evaluators, probes, prompts, and report suppression.

classification_rules.requirements: No hard fail; weighted score >= 2.40; selective_intervention_support >= 2; counterfactual_dependency >= 2; theater_resistance >= 2.
No hard fail; weighted score >= 1.60; at least one of selective_intervention_support or counterfactual_dependency >= 2; unresolved components reported separately.
Any hard fail directly defeats the declared essential dependency, or weighted score < 1.00 with adequate measurement power.
Insufficient power, failed matching, unresolved measurement validity, conflicting interventions, or weighted score from 1.00 to 1.59 without decisive hard fail.

analysis_requirements.dependency_rule: Correlated indicators sharing causal ancestry must not be multiplied as independent evidence.

permitted: Evidence increased or decreased that the declared mechanism is preserved at the specified abstraction level.
A particular indicator is or is not specific to the declared mechanism.
A result is better explained by a generic capacity, alternative pathway, or measurement artifact.
forbidden: Mechanism preservation establishes sentience, personhood, identity, or moral status.
